responses.prompt: Was rewatching the Seinfeld marble rye episode — the bit where Jerry rips the rye out of that old lady's hands on the street is so unhinged. What was the plan for the rye once he got it?
responses.response:
The plan was to replace a marble rye that George's parents (the Costanzas) had brought to a dinner at the Rosses' house.

Here's the setup: George's father, Frank, brought a marble rye as a gift when the Costanzas went to dinner with Susan's parents. But the Rosses never served it or acknowledged it, which offended Frank, so on the way out he took the rye back with him ("I brought it, I take it back").

This became a problem for George because he wanted to smooth things over with Susan's parents. The solution was to sneak a replacement rye back into the Ross house so it would seem like the original was there all along.

Jerry was tasked with getting a marble rye, but the local bakery was sold out—the last one was bought by the elderly woman on the street. Jerry tried to buy it from her, she refused, and that's when he ended up wrestling it away from her, telling her "Shut up, you old bag!"

So the whole absurd mugging was in service of getting the rye up to Susan's parents' apartment, where George planned to hoist it up on a fishing line to the window so he could pretend it had been there the whole time.

Meanwhile George, unable to get out the front door with the rye in time, ends up trying to reel the loaf up to the window with a fishing pole, which is exactly the sight that greets the Rosses when they return.

Classic Seinfeld structure: an absurdly elaborate scheme to fix a tiny social slight, and every moving part fails at the worst possible moment.

responses.prompt: Was rewatching the Seinfeld marble rye episode — the bit where Jerry rips the rye out of that old lady's hands on the street is so unhinged. What was the plan for the rye once he got it?
responses.response:
The plan was pure George Costanza damage control. Earlier in the episode, Frank and Estelle brought a marble rye from Schnitzer's to dinner at the Rosses' (Susan's parents), and when the Rosses never served it or acknowledged it, Frank was so offended that he swiped it back on the way out the door.

George is horrified the Rosses will notice the rye is missing and realize his father stole it back, so he hatches a scheme: get an identical replacement rye and sneak it *into* the Rosses' house so they'll just assume it was there all along.

Jerry's job was to buy the replacement rye from Schnitzer's and deliver it while George kept the Rosses occupied. Unfortunately, the old lady (Mabel Choate) buys the last marble rye right in front of him, refuses to sell it, and Jerry — being "a hungry guy," as he puts it — just yanks it away and bolts ("Shut up, you old bag!").

The plan falls apart in the execution: Jerry gets delayed, George can't get downstairs to let him in, so Jerry ends up attaching the rye to a fishing pole so George can reel it up through the window — right as the Rosses walk back in and catch him hauling a loaf of bread up the side of their building.

Bonus chaos: Kramer's hansom cab horse, Rusty, contributed to the delays after Kramer fed him a case of Beef-A-Reeno, with predictably gassy results for the Rosses' carriage ride.

As for the plan — it was classic Costanza overcomplication:

1. **The setup:** Frank and Estelle brought a marble rye to dinner at the Rosses' (Susan's parents). When the Rosses failed to serve it, Frank was so offended he secretly took it back on the way out.
